subclassFeature.name: College of Blades
entries: The College of Blades is among the flashiest and most theatrical of bard traditions — instead of the gentle entertainment of music or oratory, they opt for thrilling displays of swordsmanship and knifework. Blade bards may engage in any performance that seeks to awe and impress with gleaming weaponry, from knife throwing to sword swallowing to staged acrobatic duels.

Blade bards are not peerless warriors, not necessarily; rather, they are jugglers, entertainers, and daredevils whose chosen art demands ever-greater risks. They may come from any background, and are often fiercely independent, chaotic in their relationships with law and society even if they practice fierce internal discipline for their dangerous trade.

It is highly recommended to pair the College of Blades with {@link these Variant Two-Weapon Fighting rules|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-MpAZdyDDY7vb9Hzu14n}. Among other changes, these rules allow two-weapon fighting without using your bonus action.
Note that throughout, the College of Blades gets abilities related to attacking with melee weapons. It says nothing about making "melee weapon attacks"; i.e., thrown weapons work just fine.

entries.name: College of Blades Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: Rumor has it you played with a knife as a baby instead of a rattle.
Nobody has ever been able to find every {@item dagger|PHB} you keep hidden on your person.
You polish all your blades to a mirror finish, and then use them to style your hair.
When you're bored, you tend to juggle {@item dagger|PHB|daggers}, or challenge companions to sword-swallowing contests.
Your hands and arms are covered in scars from the effort of learning your trade.
Your skin is flawless, with nary a scar or scratch.

subclassFeature.name: Blade Master
entries: When you join the College of Blades at 3rd level, you gain the following benefits.

items: You can use a melee weapon you are holding as a spellcasting focus.
When you make a ranged attack with a weapon that has the thrown property, you can draw another weapon as part of the attack.
When you make a weapon attack using a weapon with the finesse property, you can use the average of your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers in place of your Dexterity modifier

entries.name: A Quick Example
entries: If your Dexterity is 14 (+2) and your Charisma is 18 (+4), you would have a +3 to attack and damage rolls. Remember to always round down.

subclassFeature.name: Blade Tricks
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn several techniques for overwhelming your opponents with skilled bladework. You can use each of these a number of times equal to the average of your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers, regaining all uses after completing a long rest. When you use a Blade Trick, you may choose to empower it by expending a spell slot. You can use each Blade Trick only once per round. When your Blade Tricks call for a saving throw, you may choose the DC to be either your spell save DC or calculated as 8 + your proficiency bonus + the average of your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers.

entries.name: Unseen Dagger
items: As a bonus action when you hit an enemy within 30 feet with an attack, you may make an attack with a melee weapon. If it is against a different target, you have advantage on the attack roll. If it hits, the attack does {@scaledamage 1d6|1-5|1d6} bonus damage. If you empower this trick with a spell slot, the damage increases by 1d6 per slot level, up to a maximum of 5d6 added damage for a 5th-level slot.

entries.name: Flashing Parry
items: As a reaction when an enemy would hit you with a melee attack while you are wielding a melee weapon, you may add your Charisma modifier to your AC until the end of the turn. If you empower this trick with a spell slot, the AC bonus increases by 1. It increases by 1 again if the slot was 3rd level or higher, and by 1 again if the slot was 5th level or higher.

entries.name: Fan of Knives
items: As an action, you may conjure a spray of blades which strike up to three creatures of your choice within 30 feet. Each target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@scaledamage 2d4|1-9|1d4} piercing damage on a failure or half as much on a success. If you empower this trick with a spell slot, the damage increases by 1d4 per slot level.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Flourish
entries: Also at 6th level, anytime you use a {@subclassFeature Blade Tricks|Bard||Blades|SMRBards|3||Blade Trick}, you may grant a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} as part of the same action.

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Bladework
entries: Starting at 14th level, when you use a {@subclassFeature Blade Tricks|Bard||Blades|SMRBards|3||Blade Trick} while you have no uses of {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} left, you regain one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1}.

subclassFeature.name: College of Bravura
entries: The College of Bravura is for bards who are refined, elegant, and perhaps a touch arrogant. Some have received training in the swashbuckler's arts, while others naturally exude an easy confidence. But all bravura bards have decided to set aside a little of their own ego, in favor of lending it to their allies.

Bravura bards come from any walk of life; indeed, detractors may assume all bards start their careers in this college. But the College of Bravura requires a certain swagger, which tends to draw humans, particularly, to its practice.

entries.name: College of Bravura Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: When asked to perform, your first instinct is always to sing a sea shanty.
You always vault over objects when running, even if there isn't any real need to do so.
You consider it a personal failure anytime you're not the best-dressed in a room.
Your weapons are always polished to a mirror sheen.
You keep a treasure map in your boot pocket.
You bow to your audience after winning a fight.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you join the College of Bravura at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with firearms and {@item buckler|SMRBards|bucklers}, and one skill of your choice out of {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, and {@skill Persuasion}. If you are already proficient in all three, you gain proficiency in any skill of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Keen Eye
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can encourage your allies with a master swordsman's keen eye and deft strike. For 1 minute after an ally expends one of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} dice, their attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

Additionally, as a reaction after a creature within 30 feet scores a critical hit, you may expend one use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1}, rolling the die three times and adding the result to the damage dealt.

subclassFeature.name: Nimble Step
entries: Also at 3rd level, when you grant {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} to an ally, they may use their reaction to move 10 feet without provoking attacks of opportunity.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Turn the Tide
entries: Starting at 14th level, anytime an ally within 30 feet scores a critical hit, you recover one expended use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1}. In addition, once per turn when you miss with an attack on your turn, you may expend a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} and add the result to your attack roll. If this causes the attack to hit, it becomes a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: College of the Canvas
entries: The College of the Canvas is for bards who channel their magic through art, painting reality to match their vision. A canvas bard sees the world not as it is, but as they wish it to be — and then makes that happen. Their artistic training and natural talent is able to tap into something fundamental about the world, and allow them to reshape it.

Canvas bards usually come from some degree of comfort — artistic skill rarely comes cheaply. They can sit anywhere on the normal axes of alignment, since imposing one's will on the world can be done in a chaotic or lawful fashion, and may be altruistic or profoundly selfish.

entries.name: College of the Canvas Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this subclass to choose from.

rows: All of your clothes are stained and paint-spattered.
Even your mundane paintings sometimes move, winking or growling at onlookers.
You have a bad habit of accidentally drawing a paintbrush instead of a weapon.
You insist on wearing a lopsided floppy hat.
You sometimes paint your companions without their permission.

subclassFeature.name: Artistic Genius
entries: When you join the College of the Canvas at 3rd level, you hone your skill with brush and ink. You gain proficiency with {@item painter's supplies|PHB}, or another tool of your choice if you're already proficient. Anytime you make a check to use your {@item painter's supplies|PHB}, you can add your Charisma modifier to the result.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Paints
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can paint a spell you know in a way that makes it usable by others. As an action, you can quickly begin to paint a change into the world. Select one creature that can see you within 60 feet and then expend a number of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} dice to select one of the effects below:

items: {@b 1+ dice:} The target gains or loses a number of hit points equal to one roll of all the {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard||1} dice expended.
{@b 2 dice:} The target gains or loses one level of {@condition exhaustion}.
{@b 3 dice:} The target gains or loses the {@condition blinded}, {@condition grappled}, or {@condition poisoned} condition.
{@b 4 dice:} The target is {@condition charmed} by you or {@condition frightened} of you.
{@b 5 dice:} The target falls asleep, and can be awoken as an action or when it takes any damage.

entries: The effect occurs at the start of your next turn, and you must {@condition concentration||concentrate} on the painting (as if you were {@condition concentration||concentrating} on a spell). The target can attempt a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, preventing the effect on a success. For ongoing effects, an unwilling creature can reattempt the Charisma saving throw at the end of each of its turns to end the effect.

subclassFeature.name: Representational Rituals
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to paint your ideas into reality. You can cast a ritual by painting the spell onto a canvas or other flat surface. You can cast a bard spell you don't know in this way if you expend a number of Bardic Inspiration uses equal to the spell's level.

subclassFeature.name: Life Imitates Art
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can paint entire creatures into existence. When you finish a long rest, you can prepare a special set of pigments, suitable for casting one of the following spells: {@spell summon animal spirit|SpellsThatDontSuck}, {@spell summon archon|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell summon fell machine|SoManySpells}, {@spell summon grave spirit|SpellsThatDontSuck}, {@spell summon monstrosity|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell summon ooze|KibblesTasty:CC}, {@spell summon plant|KibblesTasty:CC}, and {@spell summon swarm|KibblesTasty:CC}.

You can spend 10 minutes painting to cast the chosen spell, which does not expend a spell slot. The spell casts at the highest level you're able to cast up to a maximum of 5th level. If you use your {@subclassFeature Potent Paints|Bard|PHB|Canvas|SMRBards|3} while {@condition concentration||concentrating} on this spell, you are able to maintain {@condition concentration} on both simultaneously, but when you lose {@condition concentration}, both the spell and {@subclassFeature Potent Paints|Bard|PHB|Canvas|SMRBards|3} end.
You can only have one set of special pigments prepared at a time, and they are consumed when you use them.

If you'd rather not look up a whole pile of new spells for this feature, you can instead allow any {@condition concentration} spell of 3rd level or lower that summons one creature with a scaling stat block.

subclassFeature.name: Art Imitates Life
entries: Also at 6th level, you can spend 1 minute to paint a nonmagical object into existence. The object must cost 10 gp or less, weigh 5 pounds or less, and be a nonmagical object you have previously seen. It disappears after 10 minutes or when it takes or deals any damage.

subclassFeature.name: Painter of Worlds
entries: Starting at 14th level, your art is able to fully reshape reality. You learn the {@spell wish} spell, which you can only cast by painting. You must spend 1 hour painting to cast the spell, which doesn't expend a spell slot. Once you cast it, you must finish a long rest before you can cast it again.

Initially, you can only use {@spell wish} to duplicate the effects of a spell of 7th level or lower. When you reach 15th level, you can use it to duplicate the effects of a spell of 8th level or lower. When you reach 17th level, there are no restrictions on how you can use it. If you cast {@spell wish} through this feature in a way that would cause you to permanently lose the ability to do so, you instead recover after 1 week.

baseitem.name: Buckler
entries: A buckler is a small shield, typically made of metal, which is held in the fist rather than strapped to the arm. A buckler only grants a bonus of +1 to its wielder's armor class, but can be donned or doffed with a free object interaction.
Bucklers function as shields for purposes of feats and other rules concerning shields, and any character proficient with shields is also proficient with bucklers.
